VoiceInput Project

Fast Wayland Dictation

Version 0.1.0
Lightweight C-based tool for Linux desktop productivity.
C11 / PortAudio Gemini 2.5 Flash Lite Wayland / Niri

User Workflow

Speak.
Process.
Inject.

A simple, effective batch pipeline for transcribing your voice into any application.

The Journey of a Voice

Sequence of Operations

1
Trigger
Hotkey press detected by libinput
0.1s
2
Capture
16kHz audio streamed to in-memory ring buffer
Active
3
Transcribe
Audio sent to Gemini API in batch
~2s
4
Refine
AI removes fillers and adds punctuation
~1s
5
Inject
Text typed into active window via wtype
Final

System Stack

Built on Solid Pillars

VoiceInput Engine
Audio I/O
(PortAudio)
AI Core
(Gemini API)
Display
(Wayland)
Input
(libinput)

Core Capabilities

Why VoiceInput?

Native Audio

Directly sends high-quality WAV to Gemini, skipping transcription latency.

Local History

Every dictation is saved in a local SQLite database for retrieval.

Wayland Ready

Native support for Niri and Sway compositors via wtype.

Under the Hood

Data Flow Diagram

Mic Capture
Ring Buffer
Base64 Encode
Gemini API

Audio is converted from float32 to int16 before being packaged into a WAV container.

Operation Modes

Multiple Ways to Run

Recommended

Daemon Mode

Press-and-hold Super+Space. Recording starts on press and transcribes on release.

Versatile

FIFO Mode

Trigger via /tmp/voiceinput-trigger. Easy to integrate with any script.

Ready to Speak?

Get Started with VoiceInput

Check out the source code and configuration guide to start your voice-first desktop journey.
